reviews.review_title: A previous headliner for a reason...
reviews.review_description:
back when I bought my quest 2 in 2020 this was the game being showcased around the display and it caught my attention enough to give it a try...while I haven't played it in quite a while I still recommend it to people and even just gifted it to someone over the holidays because I still think it's a great experience in vr...in short think something sort of like metal gear solid but if snake was in a kayak, lol, sounds a little goofy but once you're in it you'll see how well it works and I feel is worth it for both new users to vr since there's little chance of motion sickness, and for vr veterans just because it's still such a unique experience...

My review scale*
5- Amazing game, while still might not be perfect any flaws are forgivable, is well worth your time
4- Good game, might have a few minor issues or missed opportunities keeping it from being better, but is very playable and still an enjoyable experience
3- Mid tier game, not great but not awful, these games can go either way depending on who you are or what you want or expect out of the game
2- Not a very good game, but not completely broken or unplayable, you might really need to want to play this game to get any enjoyment out of it
1- Awful game, broken or unplayable, I don't see these games being for anyone unless significant updates fix the game

*This is an overly simplified review scale since meta does not allow for half stars so in some cases I may round a game up or down depending on a case by case scenario, 
my reviews are always honest and in no way swayed by anybody else and regardless if I received a game for free or not, these ratings and reviews are however part of a 
campaign for us as consumers to be better about reviewing games and helping bring the games we love the attention they deserve.
Sooooooo good!

From start to finish, the gameplay felt smooth, the mechanics worked really well in VR, and everything flowed naturally.

Honestly, nothing to complain about at all. I played it all the way through even though it took me some time due to other game distractions but enjoyed every minute.

Easy recommendation.

reviews.review_title: one of my favorite quest games
reviews.review_description: Sitting in bed, legs extended like your in a real kayak... One of my favorite games i come back to every year!!

For a kayak only game it was really cool what you were able to do and I only knock off a star for the graphics but it still looked appealing. This game is a must own plain and simple. I dont really have any issues to bring up, the story isnt over the top, the dialogues come off professional and ive never played a military game like this before so it was all new to me. You'll get a good workout with your arms,  though I prefer standing in VR it was still entertaining.  I thought the devs did a great job! Add it to your collection if you're able.

Bold idea, brilliantly executed. A perfect example of just how much cinematic quality and action could be squeezed out of the first Quest. An intense, gripping story — it genuinely felt like being inside a high-budget movie.
Won't lie, this is a very fun game with a lot of stuff to do. This game is worth every penny.
Would’ve been a very good game if you were in a boat and being able to walk, but really without being able to walk since I’ve last played it it’s not really fun if you can’t walk

So an FPS where you never leave your kayak and also, ideally,  never shoot anyone. Kinda weird, but in the end, it was amusing. It would not be my first recommendation... or even in the top five, but sooner or later, you run low on quality single-player campaign games.  That right there is where this baby comes in for a little of that could have been better, but you know everything worked, it looked decent, and all around, it was mildly entertaining. Just get it on sale and it's worth it.

If your looking for a fast paced FPS, this isn't it. This is more of a stealth game although there are guns your penalized for killing. This makes each encounter more tense as you try to sneak your way along. Great story and good graphics too 👍
I wasted 30 dollars on a game for it to just be kayak gun and no shooting pretty much it’s horrible
I think it is a good game with the snipers and the sub-machiene guns but why does are character have a kyack for legs
Some parts on a mission are hard or fun! It was fun riding on a kayak while being stealthily! The game is very fun, i wish I could make this 10 stars!

You will understand how long you are already playing the game when your battery will die 😯
 I can definitely recommend this game.
Game looks very realistic. Tasks are well designed. Enemies looks scary. You can use different approach to solve the levels. 
Only the handling guns are little weird. On the other hand, you are not using them often if you are good in stealth.

I really love this game, it made me feel like a real soldier in the water, the movement feels fluid and smooth like I’m actually paddling on a real kayak and it’s funny to see bad guys with big guns not able to stop a guy on a kayak
If u love stealth war shooters this is it I love this game I've been playing it for 2 months so far and I am still having fun on it it's worth the price

reviews.review_title: Perfect sitting game, and very immersive
reviews.review_description: Love this game - my only complaint - I wish there were more games like this. One of the few games I haven't refunded.

I found this game to be incredible immersive. A combination of sitting down and being in the kayak made it feel totally realistic. As close as you can get to kayaking behind enemy lines without actually being there.

This game would have been enjoyable just paddling around. Add in the tasks, stealth and action and you have a truly gripping game unlike any others I’ve played on Quest.

He is half canoe and half mans. He lost his legs in The Great War. They ran out of fresh leg at every hospital so he settled for one big canoe leg, and a lonely life at sea.

His story inspire. He made the most of it by being skilled at crawling on lakes. He even invented his own tool for crawling- that’s right! He was the very first inventor of oar. And he can even crawl on ponds like a sneaky little snake.

So when the president was told “oh no there is war in the Great Russian Pond,” president laughs and says “I know just the man for the job.” That man is you, when you buy this game to become legend of Canoe-Man.

I absolutely loved playing Phantom Coverts Ops! The gameplay was incredibly immersive, the graphics were stunning, and the level design was top-notch. The ability to navigate the game in a kayak added a unique element that I haven't experienced before in a VR game.

I would highly recommend the developers consider creating a new project called Sniper Black Ops with PvP options. The idea of being able to play as a stealthy sniper in intense player versus player battles sounds incredibly exciting. With the same level of attention to detail and innovative gameplay mechanics as seen in Phantom Coverts Ops, I have no doubt that Sniper Black Ops would be a huge hit among VR gamers.

Overall, Phantom Coverts Ops has set a high standard for VR stealth games, and I can't wait to see what the developers come up with next!

This game has been a lot of fun to play though it is pretty easy and the campaign is only around 5 hours long. It took me a little while to get used to the kayak but once you master it,it is quite enjoyable. Even though it isn’t a very long campaign,it definitely has some replay value. It offers a couple different mode and you can go back and replay levels after you complete them. After each mission you are scored on the speed you completed the level,your accuracy,how many high value targets you hit and whether you were spotted or not. This isn’t a shoot everyone type of game,you’re encouraged not to make unnecessary kills..i If you don’t mind the short campaign,I would definitely recommend this game. If you don’t think it is worth the normal price,I would pick it up when it goes on sale

I’m both a former navy seal impersonator and an Olympic kayaker wannabe and this game seems accurate in both regards.

Well I played Snake Eater and the Splinter Cell games and was really good at them. And I think this game is better.

Don’t worry about graphics bro. This game looks fine. Real fine.

Two thumbs up. From a pro. Of something or other probably.

Good job. Real pro.

reviews.review_title: It’s a good game but read the descriptio
reviews.review_description: I got it thinking it would be a stand up, it’s now but still a good Game
I had a blast with this game. We need a second one!!!!

Such a great stealth game!  I’m an old school fan of Splinter Cell and this game me the same vibes…but in a kayak!  Great graphics for the time, controls were well-executed and there was a lot of freedom in how you wanted to complete each mission.  My only regret is that I bought this game two years ago during a sale and just got around to playing it now.  Quite a meaty campaign and some really cool character models thrown in towards the end credits.  Another solid game from NDreams.

developer_response.body: Hey Tomfoolery444 (great name by the way)! Thank you so much for the review, we're so happy you enjoyed Phantom: Covert Ops, we're just sorry you didn't play it sooner. It's great to hear you enjoyed the tactical kayak gameplay and the freedom the game provides :-)

reviews.review_description: Very good game but who was helping us all along if Leon was dead for a week?

developer_response.body: Thanks for the five star review marluc_11! We're glad you enjoyed Phantom: Covert Ops.

reviews.review_title: Update: RESOLVED w. latest OS update…
reviews.review_description:
I really wanted this game to work. I can do some fiddling & tweaking if necessary but the info was too sparse & devs provided no update support for Quest 3 apparently.

Long story short, while trouble shooting the no save game progress bug, I went over the two hour game play limit. (I only played the first mission three times to test if any fix would create save points but they didn’t)

I am guessing here, because they don’t have the business or legal courtesy of explaining the reason for the refund denial. Basically, I am being punished for wanting to get the game to work properly and doing their work for them instead of immediately returning it.

So to others, I suggest do not buy at any price. Not even one penny. This will be my new Meta policy. Install, run, basic troubleshoot…inside 30 minutes refund or any review complaints & I won’t buy.

ETA: 19APR26 Latest Quest OS update is allowing the game to save! So it is now playable.. It was an OS issue but as Quest shutdown the publisher at the time, no game updates were forthcoming. But if it’s for sale & discounted, I can recommend. It is fun and you can have save points to continue later. This issue is why I only gave it 4 stars.

developer_response.body: Hi Tae_Po, we're really sorry you experienced the Quest 3 save issue. Although not ideal, we have identified a workaround that allows to carry on from where you left off. If you navigate to - Settings > Storage> Find [insert game] > Delete app data (this is the left of the two options available). We hope this helps you, or any others experiencing the same issue.

reviews.review_description: You cannot save on Quest 3 and have to start from Scratch again every time you reload the game.

developer_response.body:
Hey wesblanko, we're sorry you've been experiencing the save issue on Quest 3 and really appreciate the 5 star review despite this! We have identified a workaround for the issue:

If you navigate to - Settings > Storage> Find [insert game] > Delete app data (this is the left of the two options available).

This should allow you to carry on from where you left off. If this doesn't work please reach out on support@ndreams.com and we will try our best to help.

reviews.review_description: you have to start ALLLLL the way from the very beginning when you die or turn the game off! i completed the 1st mission cut my game off and had to start from the beginning! it says auto save but thats a lie and this thing is making it hard for me to get a refund back! i do not want this game anymore!

developer_response.body:
Hi Julaibib, we're really sorry you've had to deal with the save issue on Quest 3. We wanted to respond to let you know we have identified a workaround for the save issue - 

If you navigate to - Settings > Storage> Find [insert game] > Delete app data (this is the left of the two options available).

You should only need to do this once and will be able to continue from your last save. We know this isn't ideal, but we hope it helps

reviews.review_description:
The game itself has you in a boat all the missions and you can lost easily like I don’t wanna be in a boat the whole game make a change for once and the game is boring👎😡

Don’t recommend👎

developer_response.body: I'm so sorry to hear the game didn't live up to your expectations. Thank you for giving your honest feedback on the game.

reviews.review_description: I have done every thing I know of to save my progress with no luck. The game play is awesome

developer_response.body:
[UPDATED RESPONSE] Hi there, I wanted to let you know we have a workaround for the Quest 3 save issue.

If you navigate to - Settings > Storage > Find Phantom: Covert Ops > Delete the app data (the left of the two options).

We hope this means you get to enjoy the game from your save point rather than having to start again.

[ORIGINAL RESPONSE] We're really happy to hear you're enjoying the gameplay. It sounds like you're experiencing the save issue on Quest 3 - this is being looked into by the team, but unfortunately we don't have a timescale for the fix just yet.

reviews.review_description: Made me very dizzy everytime time I play

developer_response.body: We're sorry to hear you're feeling dizzy when playing Phantom: Covert Ops. We'd suggest adjusting some of the comfort settings in options to see if they help improve your experience.

reviews.review_title: This is not a boring game! Replayable!
reviews.review_description: I loved it. There are only like 7 levels but they are really good, especially for the price! This is so fun as you get to boat around a destroyed base and spy on the enemy. You aren't supposed to kill anyone, but you can. The boat factor is really cool, movement is accurate, and the vr aspect is great. I don't wanna say too much, but it is super fun. I'll play this multiple times.

reviews.review_title: Great game, but I cant play it anymore
reviews.review_description: I was having so much fun, then it struck. I quit for 2 hours and when i got back on. It said synching. I have been unable to play the game even though I payed the full price. It was a great game and a very good one based on stealth games, which I don’t usually like. It takes the “sitting games are boring” and flips it, a wonderful game, but I just literally cannot play it. I really need help and I feel scammed in a sort of way even though it is not the devs fault.

developer_response.body:
We're so sorry to hear you're experiencing this issue, but it does sound like an issue we have encountered before (that can affect any game on the Quest platform). Hopefully this may help you recover your save...

The most common fix is to fully shutdown the headset, charge the headset for about 15 minutes, then restart and it should re-sync once rebooted. A sync shouldn’t take long, if it appears to still be stuck give it another restart and try again. One final thing you could try would be to factory reset your headset and reinstall your profile and games from scratch with a fresh install. 

There's some evidence that by allowing games to sync once you've closed the application, before fully shutting down the headset entirely at the end of a gaming session, that this will reduce the chances of the syncing issue happening for your games. By shutting down a headset, or losing power to the headset, before a sync has completed fully to the online cloud servers, this can cause a sync error where a game keeps trying to complete a failed sync that is only partially uploaded. It seems like this is a fairly common issue with the Cloud server at the moment that Meta are aware of.

As the cloud server system is operated by Meta, please escalate the issue with their support team if it persists and they may be able to offer more troubleshooting advice.

reviews.review_description: Used the app less than an hour, the motion sickness is unbarable for me. Hence wanted to get a refund. Meta sends to oculus, oculus sends to space but could not succeed to have a solution...

developer_response.body:
We're sorry to hear you've not enjoyed your experience with Phantom: Covert Ops and are struggling to obtain a refund. You can find Meta's refund policy here: https://www.meta.com/en-gb/help/orders-and-returns/returns/

If you meet all the criteria, please do raise this with them. In terms of motion sickness response, you may find it beneficial to adjust the comfort fade settings in the options menu. This will allows you to alter your FOV to find the right balance for you. We hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: It feels like I got scammed￼ waste of money, $31￼

developer_response.body: We're incredibly disappointed to read that you're unhappy with your purchase. Could you elaborate on the issue you're experiencing?

reviews.review_description: The game contractors won't stop installing don't buy it on here

developer_response.body: This seems to be a review of the game Contractors...not Phantom: Covert Ops.

reviews.review_description: Moo

developer_response.body: It is disappointing to see that you didn't enjoy your time with Phantom: Covert Ops and it wasn't quite what you were looking for. Thanks for your feedback and we hope you find a suitable alternative for your personal tastes.

reviews.review_description: I gave it about an hour hoping it would be a bit like splinter cell. But you are just sitting in a canoe moving your arms to paddle to pull some levers and then exit (which isn’t even clear on where to exit). i got tired of just rowing and tried to get a refund, but realized it was part of a package deal. It just wasn’t entertaining. Went back to pop1.

developer_response.body: We're disappointed to see that our game wasn't to your liking. If you're ever unclear on your objective or the direction you need to go, taking a photo with your military binoculars or pressing the X or Y button will provide you with a waypoint marker (after the introductory section of the game).

reviews.review_title: Took me 2.5 hours to realize it’s crap
reviews.review_description: In the very beginning i was captured by the sensation of riding a kayak in VR which is awesome, but sadly it took me a little too long to realize how bad this game is actually designed. Please don’t pay 30 bucks for this. You’ll regret it like I did. I’ve haven’t really played any stealth games before so I started on the “easy” level, but after playing one hour I realized how incredibly stupid your AI enemies are, so I put it on “medium”. After playing another 45 min, I still thought it was absolutely ridiculous how you can pass by enemies at 15 feet distance and they won’t notice you and I put it to “hard”. Even on hard this game is a total joke. Because you don’t need any “stealth” to succeed. Enemies are incredibly stupid and you can just shoot them and you’ll be fine. Sadly it took me 2.5 hours to figure this out and now I can’t get my money back. A joke of a game.

developer_response.body: We're disappointed to read that you had a negative experience with the stealth aspects of Phantom: Covert Ops and that it didn't meet your expectations of what a stealth game should be. Our three difficulties are designed to give a noticeably varied experience in terms of challenge. Enemies can indeed be dispatched with weapon play, but if you are looking to take on the global leaderboard, being able to infliltrate the base without detection or combat is the only way to rack up the points needed to compete. The free DLC we have added post launch and the challenges section of the game should be a more demanding experience if you're looking for a higher level of challenge post-endgame.

reviews.review_title: Very bad motion sickness, its true.
reviews.review_description: I read the negative reviews about its intense motion sickness, but I didn't believe them.  I have never gotten dizzy in any VR game but this game is different.  It will make you dizzy and give you worst motion sickness ever.  The graphics are great, using the gun and paddles are fun, but the gameplay is not good, sorry.

developer_response.body: Thank you for taking the time to write a review. We're disappointed to see you weren't able to enjoy our game due to motion sickness. Sadly, VR concerns like this affect people in many different ways, making them difficult to develop against for the entire spectrum of players.

reviews.review_description: Bought for my husband and made him so sick....

developer_response.body: We always appreciate feedback, but it is disappointing to hear your husband wasn't able to enjoy the experience on offer in our game.

reviews.review_description: Finally...... I was genuinely regretting paying £400 quid on my quest ! This is the first time I have actually bothered to play a game on this VR head set and genuinely enjoy it. The graphics are way better than they look on a YouTube review. The game doesn't feel linear and rushed like the majority of other gimmicky garbage quest games that I have wasted my money on in the past. This game Is very very good, it has a feel of Rockstars Manhunt about it, the atmospheric surroundings are brilliantly designed. The paddling takes a little time to get used to, but when you get used to them it's great. I have literally lost hrs and hrs playing this excellent game. To the developer's please release some future DLC for this game. This idea could be used for other future titles such as a post apocalyptic new York theme (escape from new York) Escape from La had a submarine type vehicle, imagine that in VR. How about a Neon futuristic theme like blade runner (cyberpunk) on a futurist speed boat 😂these developers are capable of anything. I have had my confidence in the quest restored, Thanks guys. Please keep up the good work and make more similar games in the future, too many puzzle and fitness games. This is what I wanted from VR.

reviews.review_title: I’d like to give it zero stars
reviews.review_description: Very hard to it on oculus quest. Horrible.

developer_response.body: We're sorry, but it isn't overly clear from your review what issues you're having. Please be sure to reach out to us on support@ndreams.com for further help.

reviews.review_description: I did enjoy this to a degree, but the campaign is too short. You spend most of the time kayaking and hiding in weeds which gets a little repetitive. You have 3 different guns and I barely used any as you only need to shoot high profile enemy’s for the highest score at the end of each level. All tho you could just shoot everyone if you wanted.

I just finished this game and I have to say it's pretty amazing. I expected a 'call of duty' feel and instead I got Metal Gear Solid. The game plays out beautifully and gave me such a metal gear solid feel with it's story line and stealth element. Definitely one of my favorites on my quest. Highly recommend.
I’d like a refund

developer_response.body: We're incredibly disappointed that you didn't enjoy Phantom: Covert Ops but without feedback, we aren't able to offer assistance or provide an explanation. Please see Oculus Support for their refund policy - https://support.oculus.com/

reviews.review_description: I hate to be this guy but this game is trash.
I love stealth so this is perfect for me

Story is ok.
Guns are good.
But sitting at one spot and just looking around... well.. not for me.
And motion sickness was so bad that I had to stop playing the game after half an hour of playing.

developer_response.body: Thanks for taking the time to leave a review about your experience! If you found that your motion sickness response was hampering your enjoyment, please do try and experiment with the Comfort Fade options in the settings menu. You may be able to strike a good balance between FOV spread and visual sharpness that is more agreeable to you.

reviews.review_description: Wait why do we stay in a Canoe for the whole game?

developer_response.body: The gameplay of Phantom: Covert Ops is designed around the stealth and momentum of sneaking into an enemy base by kayak, which is at the core of the game design. It was inspired by special forces operators like the SBS, and we always strive to ensure that what our games offer is clearly communicated in both the marketing and store descriptions.

reviews.review_title: Boring, rowing and rowing and rowing
reviews.review_description:
I just looked at the rating score. Now I know read the comments. 
Game was slooooow. Just rowing a hard to steer canoe around.
Wish I did more research as the lower re iews clearly explain your stuck in this canoe for the entire game.

developer_response.body: Thank you for writing a review, it is very important that we hear customer feedback. It's disappointing to see that the game didn't meet your expectations and wasn't what you expected. We always strive to ensure that our marketing makes clear what our games provide, and the kayak is certainly a vital part of traversal within Phantom: Covert Ops.

reviews.review_description: The game is easy to learn and lots of fun to play. I’ve enjoyed it!
Amazing! Graphics is so awesome! Highly recommended!

The game is amazing but a lot of the time when playing campaign they repeat the same things as if you didn’t know him that amount of times someone swore someone else is dead (no spoilers) but overall amazing gameplay with many hours of playtime that you can easily sink yourself into
Wow just wow thats all

The kayaking mechanics are ahead of it’s time, and the stealth is challenging, but strategic. It isn’t like other stealth games, where you walk into a room, spam one button and everything dies, because you have to conserve ammo, shoot only when absolutely necessary, and I also like the Easter eggs that are hidden. This game will change the future of stealth games forever. One thing I recommend to motion sick sensitive people is to play for shorts amount of time. Luckily I am not motion sick sensitive, but it is just a recommendation to all motion sick players. Very fun and immersive overall, and I was addicted over one hour.
Can’t believe how seasick I got just 5 minutes in…!!!

developer_response.body: It's disappointing to hear you couldn't enjoy Phantom: Covert Ops because of your motion sickness. You may be able to find a nice balance for your own response in the settings as there are options for comfort fade which allows you to play with your FOV. We hope this helps!

reviews.review_description: Stealth mechanics and shooting is pretty fun. Kayak movement can sometimes be finicky, but works for the most part. I enjoyed the game up until the last two levels. The snipers are just annoying to get past and really just take you away from the action to a tedious process of getting past them. Overall, play it if you want an enjoyable 2 to 3 hours of campaign and tons of challenge levels.
Really enjoyed game but would have liked to get out of kayak!
